pattern: The six pairwise squared distances of a square form exactly two values: four equal sides and two equal diagonals that are twice the side, which holds for any point ordering.
hints: Compare points by their pairwise squared distances to avoid floating point and ordering issues.
A square's six pairwise squared distances reduce to two distinct positive numbers occurring four and two times.
Collect all six squared distances, drop a zero (degenerate) result, then check there are exactly two distinct values where the larger equals twice the smaller and the smaller is positive.

edge_inputs.description: one point shifted far breaks the square
all four points identical is not a square
two coincident points cannot form a square
rectangle that is not a square

stress.description: rotated-and-translated genuine squares plus random near-misses
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        cx, cy = rng.randrange(-50, 51), rng.randrange(-50, 51)
        a, b = rng.randrange(1, 20), rng.randrange(1, 20)
        pts = [[cx + a, cy + b], [cx - b, cy + a], [cx - a, cy - b], [cx + b, cy - a]]
        rng.shuffle(pts)
        return [pts[0], pts[1], pts[2], pts[3]]
    pts = [[rng.randrange(-30, 31), rng.randrange(-30, 31)] for _ in range(4)]
    return [pts[0], pts[1], pts[2], pts[3]]
stress.note: half exact squares (rotation vector (a,b)), half random quads
